title: Level 9.55
wikitext:
{{Image Banner|caption=After what felt like hours, you ended up here, in the musical neighborhood...|image=peace town.jpg}}{{Blurbackground|file=peace town.jpg}}{{CSS|User:阁楼福克123/Darkness.css}} {{CSS|content=html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Kavivanar'}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 9.55: "Musical Neighborhood"}} {{Class Custom Image|class=Ypsilon|bg-image=Music Chimes.webp|safety=Relatively Unsafe|security=Stable|entity=Musical Hazards, Low Entity Count|hexagon=υ}} '''Level 9.55''' is a sub-level between [[Level 9]] and [[Level 10]]. ==
Description
== {{Blurimage|file=Music Chimes.webp|caption=An image of Level 9.55}} {{Audio|file=Slow-piano-cinematic-mood-329858_(1).mp3|secrettext=PEACHES PEACHES PEACHES PEACHES-|=true|loop=true}} '''Level​‍​‌‍​‍‌​‍​‌‍​‍‌ 9.55''' appears to be an endless residential area of streets that are stuck in a never-changing, muted twilight of sorts. The sky is a monotone dull blue with no variations and thus gives no indication of the time of day or the solar cycle. The air is still and fresh, carrying a somewhat artificial-smelling blend of ozone and freshly cut grass, still there is no sign of any organic life apart from the synthetic lawns and plastic trees. The houses look like a confusing mix of different architectural styles from the last several decades; one house could be a flawless, vintage 1950s ranch, and the following a trendy minimalist style, with each of them being the same as the ones that are several streets away. The buildings themselves are structurally unstable and hazardous. A house may present a perfectly furnished, normal interior, whereas another might contain only an empty space behind the front door, a continuous hallway of endless rooms, or a fused floor plan from multiple architectural styles. Due to this extreme architectural inconsistency—resembling a digital glitch—items within the houses frequently flicker in and out of existence, rendering the gathering of resources effectively impossible. Only a few vehicles have been documented: riderless bicycles and quiet, empty mail carrier trucks that de-manifest when approached. The main, unavoidable, and defining characteristic of the level is the perpetual, faint, and distant piano music of a somber nature. This audio phenomenon is typically a simple, repetitive melody that subtly shifts in rhythm and key as an observer moves; it emanates from all directions simultaneously, producing an eerie atmosphere of resonant emptiness. The sensation of extreme isolation is pervasive, amplified by the persistent, ghostly soundtrack that serves as a constant accompaniment to the void. Wanderers in the sub-level soon understand that the sounds are not just normal background noises; they are interactive and mind-manipulating. The piano music very quietly changes to the mood of the person that is listening or it brings up personal, most of the time, negative memories and regrets. A happy wanderer could hear a jaunty tune become a sad, broken song for a long time and thus many of them leave in a hurry to get away from the heavy psychological pressure. Long-term exploration is very hardly possible because of this psychological manipulation of hearing. There are no people on the streets and streetlights shine with a sterile, unchanging energy. An unusual type of environmental hazard can be caused by a situation where two different musical scores, which come from the distant parts of the level, temporarily overlap due to distance reduction or closeness. These "harmonic distortions" result in the most severe, but also most limited in terms of space, reality tears. For example, buildings can suddenly change their colors, the concrete floor may become liquid for a few minutes, or the gravity at a particular crossroad may stop working entirely. These distortions are very risky, however, they are the only cases in which the level's unchanging environment varies drastically. The general impression is that of a deep, carefully selected kind of solitude and a tasteful old-fashioned fading. The level doesn't have the instant sort of disorder and large number of different places that one could find in some other levels, but rather it concentrates on a single, chilling visual. This is a less obvious, more psychological version, which deceives people by its neatness and nice music, and then it comes with severe psychological issues and structural paradoxes. ==
Entities
== So far, only a few entities have been observed in '''Level 9.55''', most of which are non-hostile.
=== The Residents === Vaguely humanoid silhouettes occasionally visible in living room windows or performing mundane suburban tasks when viewed from a distance. If a wanderer approaches the property line, the figure simply turns its head to look directly at them before phasing out of existence entirely. They do not attack but contribute heavily to the level's eerie atmosphere. === [[Entity 8|Hounds]] === Standard hounds can be found, though they appear strangely clean and well-groomed, like domestic pets. They roam in silent packs and behave with normal aggression. === [[Entity 3|Smilers]] === Found almost exclusively inside the void-like houses where the front door opens into absolute darkness. Their glowing eyes are sometimes mistaken for house lights.
==
Bases & Outposts
== As of now, no bases have been established in '''Level 9.55'''. ==
Entrances & Exits
== ===
Entrances
=== The main way of entering '''Level 9.55''' is by finding a piano on [[Level 9]], but wanderers have also reported coming into the sub-level via walking towards the sound of sad piano playing in any level at random. ===
Exits
===
* No-clipping through a patch of wheat will take one to [[Level 10]]. * Finding a computer and typing in "darkened_street" will take one to [[Level 9]]. * Finding the same computer and typing in "LA_city-11" will result in the person who typed in the phrase being sent to [[Level 11]] * Finding a house with a radio inside it and turning off the radio might take one to [[Level 406]] via anomalous means.
